cmd:
set -e
BIN=/var/lib/easyenclave/bin
SRC=$BIN/podman-linux-amd64
until [ -x $SRC/usr/local/bin/podman ]; do sleep 1; done
# If there's a vdc scratch disk, wait for mount-models to actually
# mount it before we write files under /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama —
# otherwise our writes land on the rootfs tmpfs and get shadowed the
# moment vdc is mounted. On VMs without vdc (GCP CP preview) there's
# no mount-models workload and this check short-circuits.
if [ -b /dev/vdc ]; then
  until mountpoint -q /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama 2>/dev/null; do sleep 1; done
fi
mkdir -p /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/bin/* $BIN/
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/conmon $BIN/
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/netavark $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/aardvark-dns $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
cp -f $SRC/usr/local/lib/podman/rootlessport $BIN/ 2>/dev/null || true
mkdir -p /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/storage /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/runroot
# /dev/shm is where podman puts its per-container POSIX shm lock
# file (libpod_lock). EE's guest rootfs may not mount tmpfs on
# /dev/shm; without it, podman fails 'failed to create 2048 locks
# in /libpod_lock: no such file or directory'. mkdir + mount idempotently.
if ! mountpoint -q /dev/shm 2>/dev/null; then
  mkdir -p /dev/shm
  mount -t tmpfs -o size=64M tmpfs /dev/shm 2>/dev/null || true
fi
# Pick storage driver based on substrate. vdc-backed ext4 supports
# native overlay (fast + space-efficient). Without vdc (GCP CP
# preview, any guest running on tmpfs rootfs), overlay-on-tmpfs
# errors out, so fall back to vfs (slower, full copy per layer, but
# works on any filesystem).
if mountpoint -q /var/lib/easyenclave/ollama; then
  DRIVER=overlay
else
  DRIVER=vfs
fi
# Write containers.conf on vdc (writable). /etc is RO on EE so we
# can't put it where podman looks by default. helper_binaries_dir
# tells podman where we staged conmon/netavark/aardvark-dns/… —
# podman probes those at startup even with --network=host.
POL=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/policy.json
# Minimum viable signature policy: trust anything. EE's attestation
# story happens one layer up (image digest pinned by the spec we
# baked); podman's own signature checking would duplicate that.
printf '%s' '{"default":[{"type":"insecureAcceptAnything"}]}' > $POL
# Podman's containers-common looks for policy.json at hardcoded
# paths (/etc/containers/, $HOME/.config/containers/). /etc and
# /root are both RO on EE, so build a fake HOME under
# /var/lib/easyenclave/.home (writable) and set HOME there in the
# dd-podman wrapper.
HOME_DIR=/var/lib/easyenclave/.home
mkdir -p $HOME_DIR/.config/containers
cp -f $POL $HOME_DIR/.config/containers/policy.json
CONF=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/containers.conf
printf '%s\n' '[engine]' 'helper_binaries_dir = ["/var/lib/easyenclave/bin"]' > $CONF
mkdir -p $HOME_DIR/tmp
printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/sh' "export HOME=$HOME_DIR" "export TMPDIR=$HOME_DIR/tmp" "export CONTAINERS_CONF=$CONF" "exec /var/lib/easyenclave/bin/podman --conmon=/var/lib/easyenclave/bin/conmon --runtime=/var/lib/easyenclave/bin/crun --storage-driver=$DRIVER --root=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/storage --runroot=/var/lib/easyenclave/ollama/.podman/runroot --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs \"\$@\"" > $BIN/dd-podman
chmod +x $BIN/dd-podman
ls -la $CONF $POL $BIN/dd-podman 2>&1 || true
cat $CONF
echo podman-bootstrap: v2 ok driver=$DRIVER conf=$CONF policy=$POL
